title: callicarpa subalbida
intro: Callicarpa subalbida, also known as the purple bell vine or white-veined purplebell, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as relieving pain, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues, due to its antioxidant and antibacterial properties. The herbal preparations of Callicarpa subalbida can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
